rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical imino-trifluoromethylation of styrenes with CF3SO2Na and benzophenone imine to give beta-CF3 alpha-imino products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) generates CF3 radical via SO2 extrusion; CF3 radical adds to styrene to give a benzylic radical that is further oxidized to a benzylic cation, which is trapped by benzophenone imine
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the styrene terminal carbon producing a benzylic radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation; benzophenone imine traps the cation, with TBD/HFIP assisting deprotonation to yield the N-diphenylmethylene amine product 5a.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 5a was not detected, indicating involvement of radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 5a was not detected, supporting a radical pathway

notes: Derivatization of 5a with PPTS in THF/H2O hydrolyzed the diphenylmethylene imine to give the primary amine 6 (3,3,3-trifluoro-1-(4-methoxyphenyl)propan-1-aminium tosylate, 82%), confirming the imine connectivity. Only TEMPO/BHT trapping data are shown on these pages; no CV, KIE, or DFT evidence reported here.
